payload.answer: Required evaluation accounting fields: token counts (quantify resource usage per operation), provider/model IDs (attribution and cost distribution), estimated price (cost projection from token usage and pricing rules), and latency statistics (p50, p90, p95) to characterize service performance. Use token_count, provider_id, model_id, estimated_price, latency_p50, latency_p90, latency_p95 as core metrics; relate token_count to operations, and pricing rules to estimated_price. Latency percentiles should be computed over a defined time window or set of samples to ensure stable assessment.
payload.decision: provide a clear explanation of required evaluation accounting fields for token accounting in a Brain memory system, including definitions, data types, and how they interact for cost/latency accounting.

entity_resolution.reason: fields describe cost and performance characteristics required for eval accounting; no single id to resolve beyond domain definitions

memory_cards.statement: Token counts quantify consumption of tokens or units used by a request or operation, enabling granular billing and quota enforcement.
Provider/model IDs identify which provider and model instance handled a request, used for attribution and cost distribution.
Estimated price is the anticipated monetary cost for a given operation, derived from token usage and pricing rules per provider/model.
Latency metrics capture response time distribution across requests, typically as p50, p90, p95 percentiles, to quantify service performance.
